summary: Question 4.1 asks whether there is a simple criterion for a sequence of vertices to be an active path of $K_n$, where active paths are a technical notion introduced in this paper as the key tool in the lollipop method. The paper itself establishes that not every Hamiltonian path starting at $c_1$ is active (e.g., in $K_6$ exactly 6 of the 120 paths starting at $c_1$ are non-active), but provides no characterisation. No follow-up work addressing this question was found in the indexed literature as of May 2026.

notes: No follow-up found. The notion of 'active path' is introduced specifically in this paper; the question is very technical and internal to the lollipop method. The paper is recent (first posted February 2025, revised October 2025) and the concept has not yet attracted visible follow-up work.
